lviv.name: Lviv
lviv.description: A UNESCO-protected historic center: Market Square, cozy cafés, and the High Castle hill. Wander the cobbled streets of the Old Town and climb to the roof of Lviv City Hall.

bukovel.name: Bukovel and the Carpathians
bukovel.description: Ukraine's largest mountain resort. Skiing and snowboarding in winter, alpine lakes, chairlifts, and Carpathian trekking in summer.

hoverla.name: Hoverla
hoverla.description: Ukraine's highest peak (2,061 m). A one-day ascent from Zaroslyak — a must-do for every traveler.

synevyr.name: Lake Synevyr
synevyr.description: The largest mountain lake in the Carpathians, ringed by spruce forest. Right nearby is a brown bear rehabilitation center.

kamianets.name: Kamianets-Podilskyi
kamianets.description: An old fortress perched above the Smotrych river canyon. One of Ukraine's most picturesque strongholds — walk across the Castle Bridge.

khotyn.name: Khotyn Fortress
khotyn.description: A mighty medieval stronghold on the banks of the Dniester. Frequently used as a filming location for historical movies and festivals.

chernivtsi.name: Chernivtsi
chernivtsi.description: The former residence of Bukovinian metropolitans (UNESCO) and a cozy Austrian-style center. Ukraine's own "Little Vienna".

bakota.name: Bakota
bakota.description: A submerged village and cliffside monastery above the Dniester canyon. Perfect scenery for sunrise photography.

uman.name: Sofiyivka Park, Uman
uman.description: One of Europe's most beautiful arboretums: waterfalls, grottoes, an underground river, and romantic alleys.

kyiv.name: Kyiv
kyiv.description: St. Sophia's Cathedral, the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, Andriyivskyi Descent, and the Dnipro embankments. The heart of the country, with a thousand years of history.

klevan.name: Tunnel of Love, Klevan
klevan.description: A railway track completely wrapped in a green arch of trees. It looks best in fresh spring foliage or golden autumn colors.

chernihiv.name: Chernihiv
chernihiv.description: One of the oldest cities of Kyivan Rus: the Transfiguration and Borys and Hlib cathedrals, the ramparts of the old citadel, and legendary caves.

kharkiv.name: Kharkiv
kharkiv.description: Freedom Square, the largest in Europe, Shevchenko Garden, and a vibrant university atmosphere.

khortytsia.name: Khortytsia, Zaporizhzhia
khortytsia.description: The largest island on the Dnipro and the cradle of Cossack history. A museum of Zaporizhian Cossack history and a reconstructed Sich fortress.

askania.name: Askania-Nova
askania.description: The world's oldest steppe biosphere reserve. An open-air zoo where zebras and bison roam free on the steppe.

odesa.name: Odesa
odesa.description: The Potemkin Stairs, Deribasivska Street, the opera house, and the seaside promenade. Ukraine's summer capital, famous for its wit.

akkerman.name: Akkerman Fortress
akkerman.description: One of Ukraine's largest fortresses, on the shore of the Dniester estuary in Bilhorod-Dnistrovskyi. Over 2,000 years of history.

optymistychna.name: Optymistychna Cave
optymistychna.description: The world's longest gypsum cave — over 260 km of passages beneath the fields near the village of Korolivka, a Guinness World Record holder. Its crystal labyrinths are stunning, but a descent is only possible with a guide and proper gear.

oleshky_sands.name: Oleshky Sands
oleshky_sands.description: Europe's largest sand expanse, nicknamed "Ukraine's Sahara": genuine dunes up to 5 meters high amid the steppe. In summer the sand heats up to +70 °C, so spring or early autumn is best.

dnistrovskyi_canyon.name: Dniester Canyon
dnistrovskyi_canyon.description: One of Europe's largest canyons — 250 km of winding rocky banks along the Dniester, with rock formations hundreds of millions of years old. Ideal for kayaking, cycling tours, and camping right above the water.

tustan.name: Tustan
tustan.description: A unique 9th–16th century cliff fortress near the village of Urych: only grooves and notches carved into giant boulders remain of the wooden city. No European equivalent of this construction exists.

pidhirtsi.name: Pidhirtsi Castle
pidhirtsi.description: A 17th-century Renaissance palace-fortress with Italian terraces, hailed as the finest example of palace architecture in Eastern Europe. Wrapped in legends of the White Lady, it's part of the "Golden Horseshoe of Lviv Region".

olesko.name: Olesko Castle
olesko.description: A 14th-century castle on a hill amid marshes, birthplace of King Jan III Sobieski. Inside is a rich collection of old art; outside, a French-style park with sculptures.

zolochiv.name: Zolochiv Castle
zolochiv.description: A model 17th-century bastion fortress with the Great and Chinese Palaces. In the courtyard stand the mysterious "Templar stones" with still-undeciphered inscriptions.

medzhybizh.name: Medzhybizh Castle
medzhybizh.description: A white 16th-century stronghold at the confluence of the Southern Buh and the Buzhok, nicknamed "the ship" for its shape. One of the best-preserved fortresses of Podillia, with a museum and knightly festivals.

svirzh.name: Svirzh Castle
svirzh.description: A romantic 15th–17th century castle above a pond, surrounded by a park and a drawbridge. So cinematic that "D'Artagnan and the Three Musketeers" was filmed here.

rozhirche.name: Rozhirche Cave Monastery
rozhirche.description: A two-tiered cave monastery carved into sandstone rock back in the 13th–14th centuries. Standing in the forest above the village, it's a quiet, nearly empty alternative to the popular castles.

kryvorivnia.name: Kryvorivnia
kryvorivnia.description: A Hutsul village on the Cheremosh river, where Parajanov filmed "Shadows of Forgotten Ancestors" and Franko and Hrushevsky spent their summers. Authentic life still thrives here: traditional homesteads, a wooden church, and museums inside village houses.

lumshory.name: Lumshory
lumshory.description: A mountain village known for its sulfur springs and bathing in wood-heated cast-iron tubs, a tradition since the 17th century. Nearby are waterfalls on the Turytsia stream.

tovtry.name: Podillia Tovtry
tovtry.description: Ukraine's largest national park — a rocky ridge that is the remnant of an ancient sea's barrier reef. Canyons, caves, springs, and unique steppe flora atop limestone ridges.

mavryn.name: Mavryn Maidan
mavryn.description: A mysterious earthen structure over 100 meters in diameter — an excavated Scythian burial mound resembling a crater with "petals". Estimated at 5,000 years old, its purpose is still not fully understood.

trostianets.name: Trostianets and the Round Yard
trostianets.description: A small town with the estate of sugar magnate Kenig, where Tchaikovsky wrote his first symphony. Its main marvel is the Round Yard, an 18th-century circular "fortress" once used as a riding arena.

opishnia.name: Opishnia — the pottery capital
opishnia.description: Ukraine's unofficial pottery capital, home to the National Museum-Reserve of Ukrainian Pottery and the country's largest ceramics collection. Sit at a wheel and shape your own jug.

buzkyi_hard.name: Buzkyi Hard
buzkyi_hard.description: A granite steppe stretch of the Southern Buh, which breaks through rock formations 2 billion years old. Rafting rapids, the White Cliffs, the Turkish Table, and memories of the Cossack Hard.

nesamovyte.name: Lake Nesamovyte
nesamovyte.description: A glacial lake at 1,750 m right on the Chornohora ridge, wrapped in legends that a stone thrown into the water summons a storm. Reachable only on foot, as part of the route to Hoverla and Pip Ivan.

brebeneskul.name: Lake Brebeneskul
brebeneskul.description: Ukraine's highest-altitude lake — 1,801 m above sea level, cradled beneath the peak of the same name. The water stays ice-cold year-round, and getting here is a full-fledged alpine trek.

shypit.name: Shypit Waterfall
shypit.description: A cascading 14-meter waterfall at the foot of Mount Borzhava, at its fullest in spring. Easily reached from the village of Pylypets — a great start before climbing to the alpine meadow.

maniava.name: Maniava Waterfall
maniava.description: The tallest waterfall in the Gorgany mountains — about 20 meters through a narrow wooded gorge locals call the Slavic Falls. The trail here passes the Maniava Hermitage.

dzhuryn.name: Dzhuryn Waterfall
dzhuryn.description: Ukraine's tallest lowland waterfall — 16 meters, in a canyon near the village of Nyrkiv. It's man-made: the Dzhuryn riverbed was carved out back in the 16th century, and today people swim and camp here.

dovbush.name: Dovbush Rocks
dovbush.description: Massive sandstone boulders in the forest near Bubnyshche, with caves, stairs, and passages carved as far back as the Kyivan Rus era. Legend says outlaw Oleksa Dovbush hid his treasure here.

narcissi.name: Valley of Narcissi
narcissi.description: The world's only lowland stand of narrow-leaved daffodils — 170 hectares of white blooms near Khust. This relic wonder flowers for only two weeks a year, so timing is everything.

zacharovanyi_krai.name: Zacharovanyi Krai (Enchanted Land)
zacharovanyi_krai.description: A national park on the Vyhorlat-Hutyn ridge with volcanic rock formations of strange shapes — the Spruce Rock, the Colorful Cliffs, and the Black Bog tract. Few tourists, and plenty of genuine forest.

smotrych.name: Smotrych Canyon
smotrych.description: A looping canyon up to 50 meters deep that encircles the Old Town of Kamianets-Podilskyi. The best views are from below, along the riverside path, not from the bridge.

stilske.name: Stilske Hillfort
stilske.description: One of Europe's largest Slavic hillforts — around 250 hectares, capital of the White Croats in the 9th century. Carved caves, a temple, and dwellings survive in the rocks.

chyhyryn.name: Chyhyryn and Bohdan Khmelnytsky's Residence
chyhyryn.description: The former Hetman's capital, with a reconstructed residence of Bohdan Khmelnytsky on Castle Hill. The bastions offer a view over the Tiasmyn river and the whole Cossack countryside.

palanok.name: Palanok Castle
palanok.description: A mighty castle on a 68-meter volcanic hill above Mukachevo, never once taken by storm. Three tiers of courtyards, an 85-meter well, and a panorama over all of Zakarpattia.

nevytske.name: Nevytske Castle
nevytske.description: Romantic ruins of a 13th-century castle on a volcanic cliff above the Uzh river, surrounded by an old arboretum. Legend says it was built by the "Wicked Maiden" using chicken eggs instead of mortar.

vylkove.name: Vylkove — the Ukrainian Venice
vylkove.description: A town on the water in the Danube Delta, where canals replace streets and boats replace cars. The Danube's zero kilometer and the pelicans of the biosphere reserve are just a short trip away.

tuzly.name: Tuzly Estuaries
tuzly.description: A wild national park — a chain of saltwater lagoons cut off from the sea by a narrow sandbar. No infrastructure, but thousands of birds and completely empty beaches.

tokivskyi.name: Tokivskyi Waterfall
tokivskyi.description: Cascades of the Kamianka river over pink granite — the same stone used for Napoleon's sarcophagus. Most striking in spring, at high water; abandoned quarries lie nearby.

radomysl.name: Radomysl Castle
radomysl.description: A historical and cultural complex in a restored 19th-century water mill, built on the site of a 1612 paper mill. Inside is a unique museum of Ukrainian home icons.

korostyshiv.name: Korostyshiv Quarry
korostyshiv.description: A flooded granite quarry with crystal-clear water, rocky walls, and pines along the shore — locals call it the Zhytomyr fjord. A popular spot for diving and camping.

kachanivka.name: Kachanivka
kachanivka.description: Ukraine's largest palace-and-park ensemble — 560 hectares surrounding the Tarnovsky palace, once visited by Shevchenko, Gogol, and Repin. Alleys, ponds, an amphitheater, and the Hunchback Bridge.

baturyn.name: Baturyn Fortress
baturyn.description: The rebuilt citadel of the Hetman's capital, destroyed by Menshikov's troops in 1708. Nearby stands the Rozumovsky Palace, one of the finest examples of classicism in Ukraine.

sanzhiyka.name: Sanzhiyka
sanzhiyka.description: A village on a cliff over the sea, hiding a wide sandy beach and an old lighthouse below. A quiet alternative to Odesa's crowded resorts, an hour's drive from the city.

atlantyda.name: Atlantyda Cave
atlantyda.description: A three-tiered gypsum cave near Zavallia with rare crystals and calcite flowstone formations. Some passages are extremely narrow — only with a guide, and without fear of tight spaces.

dzembronia.name: Dzembronia
dzembronia.description: The highest-altitude village in Ivano-Frankivsk Region and a gateway to Chornohora via the Dzembronia rock outcrops. Routes to Smotrych, Pip Ivan, and Vukhatyi Kamin start here.

hutsulshchyna.name: Hutsulshchyna National Park
hutsulshchyna.description: A park around Kosiv combining beech forest, the rocky ridges of Sokilske, and living Hutsul crafts. Excellent short routes with Carpathian panoramas.

probiy.name: Probiy Waterfall
probiy.description: The most powerful waterfall in the Carpathians, right in the center of Yaremche: the Prut river breaks through an 8-meter rocky sill. Nearby are the famous souvenir market and Bear Rock.

kuyalnyk.name: Kuyalnyk Estuary
kuyalnyk.description: A salt lake whose water turns pink in summer from a microalga, with healing black mud and a resort dating to 1833. Surreal scenery with a salt crust along the shores.

kryshtaleva.name: Kryshtaleva Cave
kryshtaleva.description: A developed gypsum cave near Kryvche — the only one in the region with a guided tour route and lighting. Walls are covered in gypsum crystals, and halls bear names like "Bull's Head".

zbarazh.name: Zbarazh Castle
zbarazh.description: A 17th-century bastion castle immortalized by Sienkiewicz in "With Fire and Sword" for its legendary 1649 defense. A restored palace, ramparts, and a museum in the town center.

berezhany.name: Berezhany Castle
berezhany.description: The Sieniawski family's Renaissance castle on an island between branches of the Zolota Lypa, once called the Galician Wawel. Now atmospheric ruins with remnants of a unique castle chapel.

sataniv.name: Sataniv
sataniv.description: A small town with a 16th-century castle, Podillia's oldest fortress-synagogue, and mineral springs. Surrounded by the tovtry ridges and forest trails of the national park.

kudryntsi.name: Kudryntsi Castle
kudryntsi.description: Ruins of a 17th-century castle atop Striletska Hill above the Zbruch river — the wildest and least-visited of Podillia's castles. The climb is steep, but the valley panorama is worth it.

khrinnyky.name: Khrinnyky Reservoir
khrinnyky.description: A large reservoir on the Styr river known as the "Rivne sea": sandy spits, islands, and coves amid the forest. The region's top spot for camping, windsurfing, and fishing.

shatsk.name: Lake Svityaz
shatsk.description: Ukraine's deepest natural lake (58 m), with clear karst water and a sandy bottom — the heart of Shatsk National Park. Swimming, cycling, and pine forests all around.

kaniv.name: Kaniv Nature Reserve
kaniv.description: The Kaniv Hills — a "Ukrainian Switzerland" of ravines, oak groves, and Dnipro bluffs beside Shevchenko's grave. One of the country's oldest reserves, with a scientific nature trail.

vukhatyi.name: Mount Vukhatyi Kamin
vukhatyi.description: Ear-shaped rock outcrops on the Chornohora ridge, with views of Pip Ivan and all of Dzembronia. One of the Carpathians' best spots to watch sunrise above a sea of clouds.

shyshkovi.name: Shyshkovi Hills
shyshkovi.description: Cone-shaped limestone hills — remnants of an ancient coral reef — on the Dniester bluffs near the village of Nahoriany. Morning mist over the water makes this spot feel almost unreal.

chervonohorod.name: Chervonohorod Castle
chervonohorod.description: Two lonely palace towers amid a vanished town in the Dzhuryn canyon — the town died out, but the castle remained. 700 meters away roars Ukraine's tallest lowland waterfall.

drahobrat.name: Drahobrat
drahobrat.description: Ukraine's highest-altitude ski resort (1,400 m), beneath the peaks of Stih and Blyznytsia, reachable only by off-road vehicle. Snow lingers until May, while summer brings glacial lakes and wild blueberries.
